nl_proof: Explained:
Sentences used:
(1) John works at the hospital every day.
(2) Where does John work?
Proof steps:
(1) If X takes place some Z some Y and X is a work event and John performs X and X is typical and X is an activity then [Z, Y] is the answer  [sentence 2]
(2) The event act4 is a work event  [sentence 1]
(3) If the event act4 is an activity and the act4 takes place some Y some X and John performs the act4 and the act4 is typical then [Y, X] is the answer  [from steps 1, 2]
(4) John performs the event act4  [sentence 1]
(5) If the event act4 is an activity and the act4 takes place some Y3 some X3 and the act4 is typical then [Y3, X3] is the answer  [from steps 3, 4]
(6) The event act4 is an activity  [sentence 1]
(7) If the event act4 takes place some Y some X and the act4 is typical then [Y, X] is the answer  [from steps 5, 6]
(8) The event act4 takes place at the hospital B  [sentence 1]
(9) If the event act4 is typical then 'at the hospital B' is the answer  [from steps 7, 8]
(10) technical answer unwrap step  [from question]
(11) If the event act4 is typical then 'at the hospital B' is an answer  [from steps 9, 10]
(12) Normally the event act4 is typical  [sentence 1]
(13) 'At the hospital B' is an answer, except when the event act4 is not typical  [from steps 11, 12]
Exceptions checked and not holding:
act4 is not typical
